steps.title: Find the hourly rate for additional hours
steps.content:
The specialist charges \$160 for the first 2 hours. For a 6-hour repair, that's 4 additional hours. Since the total cost is \$360:
$$160 + (\text{rate}) \times 4 = 360$$
Solving: $(\text{rate}) \times 4 = 200$, so the hourly rate for additional hours is \$50.

steps.title: Build the function for x ≥ 2
steps.content:
The total cost for $x$ hours is the fixed \$160 charge plus \$50 per additional hour beyond 2:
$$f(x) = 160 + 50(x - 2)$$
Simplify to standard form:
$$f(x) = 50x + 60$$
That matches choice A — A is correct.
